principle.name: Set clear goals
principle.description: Every task must have a single, clear goal statement. The Architect and Coder modes need to understand WHAT before HOW.
principle.rationale: Unclear goals lead to hesitation and hallucination in downstream modes.

example.good: Add shield bash: player presses B near enemy, enemy is stunned for 1 turn, 3-turn cooldown

principle.name: Classify honestly
principle.description: Do not over-classify or under-classify complexity. SINGLE_FILE tasks should not go through Architect mode unnecessarily.
principle.rationale: Over-classification wastes time. Under-classification sends hard tasks to Coder without design.

principle.name: Track progress
principle.description: Maintain execution log of all stages, their results, and timing.
principle.rationale: Enables debugging of the pipeline itself and provides audit trail.

routing_rules.rule: Always produce a task ID before dispatching
Always write task description to plans/task_description.md before Architect handoff
Always verify Architect output exists before handing off to Coder
Always verify Coder tests pass before handing off to Playtester
On failure, route to Debugger not back to Architect

pitfall.why_problematic: Coder mode cannot make design decisions. It will hallucinate or stall. Always route MULTI_FILE, SYSTEM, and CRITICAL tasks through Architect first
Leads to Architect making wrong assumptions, Coder implementing wrong thing. Include specific file paths, expected behavior, and constraints in task description

category.name: before_dispatching
category.item: Task description is clear and specific
